eirp-offset.description: User offset of EIRP on top of algorithm choice. Integer in [-6,6]. Default: 0 dB

energy-detect-threshold.description: Modify the Energy Detect Threshold (EDT) used by the radio in making transmit decisions. The EDT is a negative value, and the value specified here is the offset from the base value of -59 dBm (e.g., 1:  -59 - 1 = -60 dBm, 10: -59 - 10 = -69 dBm). 0: Use the default EDT for this radio (AP dependent) 1 to 12: Modify EDT to the specified value (i.e., -60 dBm to -71 dBm)

interference-immunity.description: Non-Wi-Fi Interference Immunity adjusts how immune the radio will be to non-Wi-Fi interference. This level should be changed with caution. Increasing the immunity level in a healthy network will result in severe loss of performance. This should be set to a higher than default level only when there is significant degradation due to non-Wi-Fi interference. Available values: 0-16 Default value: 2

maximum-distance.description: Maximum wireless-link distance [meters]. Used to derive slot-time and ACK and CTS timeouts. 0 means use defaults: timeouts are unmodified except for outdoor mesh-radios which use 16km. Upper limits: legacy/11N AP 20MHz: 52km; 11N AP 40MHz: 24km; 11AC AP: 48km. Values above maximum supported cause the maximum to be used, below 600m defaults are used. Outdoor mesh points start up with the maximum supported until configured.

mgmt-frame-throttle-interval.description: Averaging interval for rate limiting mgmt frames from this radio in secs. 0 disables rate limiting

radar-test-mode.description: Radar Testing mode. Mode 0 indicate test mode is disabled. Mode 1 makes the AP ignore the radar (but still detects it, see counter or console log). Mode 2 is like mode 1 but sends out Channel Switch Announcement, without actually switching. This is only for internal testing.

spur-immunity.description: Spur Immunity for 5GHz radio applicable to AP-13x only. Range: 0-20. Default: 0.

tx-power.description: Maximum transmit EIRP (Equivalent Isotropic Radiated Power) in dBm by 0.1 dB step in range [-51, 51] and special values +/- 127. Further limited by regulatory domain constraints and AP capabilities.

cell-size-reduction.description: Reduce cell size by controlling Wi-Fi Rx sensitivity. Use this to manage dense deployments and to increase overall system performance/capacity by minimizing co-channel interference and optimizing channel reuse. 0: default sensitivity. 1 - 55: sensitivity reduction from default (dB).

channel-reuse-threshold.description: RX Sensitivity Threshold (dB) above noise floor (applicable only when channel reuse is static) Default: 0 - autocompute.

spectrum-load-bal-domain.description: Spectrum Load Balancing Domain name (max. 32 characters). All APs with same domain name will be considered as RF neighbors. Default: Automatically calculate RF neighborhood based on scanning

slb-threshold.description: Controls the % difference between number of clients per channels when SLB kicks in

slb-update-interval.description: Controls how often spectrum load balancing calculations are made (in sec)

csa-count.description: Number of channel switch announcements that must be sent prior to switching to a new channel.

deploy-hour.description: Configure hour-of-day for AP solution deployment. Values [0-23]. Default: N/A. Overrides Airmatch profile if a valid hour is specified.

eirp-max.description: Maximum EIRP. Further limited by regulatory domain constraints and AP capabilities. Range [1-127]

eirp-offset.description: User offset of EIRP on top of algorithm choice. Integer in [-6,6]. Default: 0 dB

energy-detect-threshold.description: Modify the Energy Detect Threshold (EDT) used by the radio in making transmit decisions. The EDT is a negative value, and the value specified here is the offset from the base value of -59 dBm (e.g., 1:  -59 - 1 = -60 dBm, 10: -59 - 10 = -69 dBm). 0: Use the default EDT for this radio (AP dependent) 1 to 12: Modify EDT to the specified value (i.e., -60 dBm to -71 dBm)

interference-immunity.description: Non-Wi-Fi Interference Immunity adjusts how immune the radio will be to non-Wi-Fi interference. This level should be changed with caution. Increasing the immunity level in a healthy network will result in severe loss of performance. This should be set to a higher than default level only when there is significant degradation due to non-Wi-Fi interference. Available values: 0-16 Default value: 2

maximum-distance.description: Maximum wireless-link distance [meters]. Used to derive slot-time and ACK and CTS timeouts. 0 means use defaults: timeouts are unmodified except for outdoor mesh-radios which use 16km. Upper limits: legacy/11N AP 20MHz: 52km; 11N AP 40MHz: 24km; 11AC AP: 48km. Values above maximum supported cause the maximum to be used, below 600m defaults are used. Outdoor mesh points start up with the maximum supported until configured.

mgmt-frame-throttle-limit.description: Max. number of mgmt frames that can come in from this radio in each throttle interval.

mgmt-frame-throttle-interval.description: Averaging interval for rate limiting mgmt frames from this radio in secs. 0 disables rate limiting

radar-test-mode.description: Radar Testing mode. Mode 0 indicate test mode is disabled. Mode 1 makes the AP ignore the radar (but still detects it, see counter or console log). Mode 2 is like mode 1 but sends out Channel Switch Announcement, without actually switching. This is only for internal testing.

spur-immunity.description: Spur Immunity for 5GHz radio applicable to AP-13x only. Range: 0-20. Default: 0.

tx-power.description: Maximum transmit EIRP (Equivalent Isotropic Radiated Power) in dBm by 0.1 dB step in range [-51, 51] and special values +/- 127. Further limited by regulatory domain constraints and AP capabilities.

ip-or-dns.description: IP address or DNS name of the Aeroscout server

airmatch-report-period.description: AirMatch report period. Range: 5-180 (3 hours) minutes. Default: 30.

ap-deploy-hour.description: Configure hour-of-day for solution deployment for all Radios of an AP. Values [0-23]. Default: N/A. Overrides Airmatch profile if a valid hour is specified.

dynamic-bw-clear-time.description: Dynamic Bandwidth Switch back to 80MHz channel after the clear time in mins if currently there is no high volumne of traffic. Default: 30.

acceptable-coverage-index.description: Acceptable Coverage AP should try to achieve on its channel. The denser the AP deployment, lower this value should be. Range: 1-10. Default: 4.

ideal-coverage-index.description: Ideal Coverage AP should try to achieve on its channel. The denser the AP deployment, lower this value should be. Range: 2-20. Default: 10.

radar-backoff-time.description: Duration in mins for blacklisting Airmatch Solver's channel after 2 consecutive Radar detections. Setting to 0 will disable the backoff window Range: 0 - 3600mins (60hrs). Default: 720mins (12hrs)

max-tx-a-mpdu-size.description: Maximum size of a transmitted aggregate MPDU. Range: 1576-65535.

max-tx-a-msdu-count-be.description: Maximum number of MSDUs in a TX A-MSDU on best-effort AC. Range: 0-15. Default 2. In both Tunnel and D-Tunnel mode TX-AMSDU disabled if 0. In Tunnel mode if non-zero, TX A-MSDU is set to this value. In D-Tunnel mode if non-zero, TX A-MSDU is enabled and set to the configured value.

max-tx-a-msdu-count-bk.description: Maximum number of MSDUs in a TX A-MSDU on background AC. TX-AMSDU disabled if 0. Range: 0-15. Default 2. In D-Tunnel mode TX A-MSDU on background AC is disabled and assigning any value has no effect.

max-tx-a-msdu-count-vi.description: Maximum number of MSDUs in a TX A-MSDU on video AC. TX-AMSDU disabled if 0. Range: 0-15. Default 2. In D-Tunnel mode TX A-MSDU on video AC is disabled and assigning any value has no effect.

max-tx-a-msdu-count-vo.description: Maximum number of MSDUs in a TX A-MSDU on voice AC. TX-AMSDU disabled if 0. Range: 0-15. Default 0. In D-Tunnel mode TX A-MSDU on voice AC is disabled and assigning any value has no effect.

supported-mcs-set.description: Comma-separated list of MCS values or ranges of values to be supported on this SSID. MCS 0-31 are supported. Default: 0-31. (16-23 are supported on AP-13x/RAP-15x/11ac APs only, 24-31 are supported on AP-32x/33x APs only)

vht-supported-mcs-map.description: Comma separated list of max supported MCS for spatial streams 1 through 8. Valid values for max mcs are 7, 8, 9 and '-' ('-' means spatial stream is not supported, and it's not supported at first spatial stream). Max mcs of a spatial stream cannot be higher than the previous stream's. If a MCS is not valid for a particular combination of bandwidth and number of spatial streams, it will not be used for Tx and Rx. Default: 9,9,9,9,9,9,9,9

certificate-lock-out.description: Configuring the number of failed attempts within 3 minute window to lockout the user. Provides ability to reduce the number of login attempts that can be made in a short time. Automatically clears the lockout after configured 'lock-out' minutes. Range: 0-10 attempts. Default: 0 (lockout of users is disabled by default).

certificate-lock-out-time.description: Configuring the number of minutes the user is locked out. The lockout is cleared without administrator intervention. Range: 1 min to 1440 max (24 hrs). Default: 3 min.

password-lock-out.description: Configuring the number of failed attempts within 3 minute window to lockout the user. Provides ability to reduce the number of passwords that can be guessed in a short time. Automatically clears the lockout after configured 'lock-out' minutes. Range: 0-10 attempts. Default: 0 (lockout of users is disabled by default).

password-lock-out-time.description: Configuring the number of minutes the user is locked out. The lockout is cleared without administrator intervention. Range: 1 min to 1440 max (24 hrs). Default: 3 min.

password-max-character-repeat.description: Configuring the maximum consecutive characters in password. Range: 0-10 max consecutive characters. Default: 0 (enforcing maximum number of consecutive character repeat is disabled by default).

min-char-difference.description: Configuring the minimum number of characters that the new password should differ from the old password.

password-min-digit.description: Configuring the minimum number of digits (0 through 9) in password . Range: 0-10 numeric digits. Default: 0 (minimum number of digits required is disabled by default).

password-min-length.description: Configuring the minimum password length requirement Range: 6-32 password length. Default: 6.

password-min-lowercase-characters.description: Configuring the minimum number of lower case (a through z)  characters in password . Range: 0-10 minimum lower case characters. Default: 0 (minimum number of lower case required is disabled by default).

password-min-special-character.description: Configuring the minimum number of special characters (!, @, #, $, %, ^, &, *, <, >, {, }, [, ], :, ., comma, |, +, ~, ` ) in password.  Range: 0-10 special characters. Default: 0 (minimum number of special character required is disabled by default, The following ( ')', '(' ;, -, space, =, /, ?) are dis-allowed).

password-min-uppercase-characters.description: Configuring the minimum number of upper case (A through Z) characters in password . Range: 0-10 minimum upper case characters. Default: 0 (minimum number of upper case required is disabled by default).

audit_period.description: Configure audit period(0-365, 0 means disabling audit-info)
